item.title: How to Plant and Grow Daikon Radish
item.description:
If you’re looking to add some zing to your fall garden, look no further than the daikon radish. With white roots that can weigh over a pound, these large radishes are low maintenance and easy to grow. While they are widely grown and used in parts of Asia, they can be grown elsewhere. Read more now.

The post How to Plant and Grow Daikon Radish appeared first on Gardener's Path.
item.source:markdown:
If you’re looking to add some zing to your fall garden, look no further than the daikon radish. With white roots that can weigh over a pound, these large radishes are low maintenance and easy to grow. While they are widely grown and used in parts of Asia, they can be grown elsewhere. Read more now.

item.title: How to Grow and Care for Baboon Flowers (Babiana)
item.description:
Baboon flowers (Babiana nervosa) are spring-blooming South African natives with spikes of fragrant, funnel-shaped flowers in shades of blue, purple, and yellow. These citrus-scented cormous perennials are ideal for rockeries, cottage gardens, and containers. Learn how to grow baboon flowers in this guide. Read more now.

The post How to Grow and Care for Baboon Flowers (Babiana) appeared first on Gardener's Path.
item.source:markdown:
Baboon flowers (Babiana nervosa) are spring-blooming South African natives with spikes of fragrant, funnel-shaped flowers in shades of blue, purple, and yellow. item.title: Learn How to Grow and Care for Gryphon Begonias
item.description:
Gryphon begonia is a shade-loving tropical hybrid prized for its glossy, silver-streaked green foliage with red undersides. The strikingly variegated leaves measure up to 10 inches across for a substantial annual, houseplant, or Zone 8 to 10 perennial. Learn how to grow and care for showy Gryphon begonia in this guide.

The post Learn How to Grow and Care for Gryphon Begonias appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: Learn How to Grow Creeping Jenny (Moneywort)
item.description:
Creeping Jenny aka moneywort is a fast-growing, colorful, and lush ground cover in areas with moist soil such as bog margins, creek banks, and pond fringes. Its spread can become aggressive if left unchecked, but its a charming addition to pots and planters. Learn how to grow creeping Jenny in this guide. Read more now.

The post Learn How to Grow Creeping Jenny (Moneywort) appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: How to Grow Dandelions for Greens, Roots, and Flowers
item.description:
You may think of dandelions as invasive weeds, but did you know that the greens, flowers, and roots are edible? Delicious and nutritious, leaves can be added to salads, flowers made into a tea, and the roots can be used as a coffee substitute. Learn how to cultivate and harvest dandelions in this guide. Read more now.

The post How to Grow Dandelions for Greens, Roots, and Flowers appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: Learn How to Grow Wild Violets in Your Garden
item.description:
Wild violets might be more subtle than their showier cultivated cousins, but they are every bit as charming. These wild little bloomers shine in purple, pink, white, and yellow, and will add easygoing color and whimsy to your garden, as well as edible blooms. Learn how to grow wild violets in this guide. Read more now.

item.title: Tips for Companion Planting in Your Herb Garden
item.description:
Herbs make powerful companion plants, and not just in your vegetable patch. With strong aromas and tastes, their pest-repelling, beneficial insect-attracting, and other benefits can extend to fellow herbs as well. Learn which ones do well planted near each other, and which don't enjoy each other's company.

The post Tips for Companion Planting in Your Herb Garden appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: How to Grow Chocolate Cosmos Flowers
item.description:
Chocolate cosmos flowers have velvety maroon petals that infuse late-day summer sunshine with the fragrance of chocolate. The species grows as a perennial in Zones 9 to 11 and can be enjoyed as an annual in cooler regions, lifting and storing the tubers at season's end. Learn how to grow chocolate cosmos in this guide.

The post How to Grow Chocolate Cosmos Flowers appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: Learn How to Grow Fountain Grass (Pennisetum)
item.description:
Ornamental fountain grass is hardy in Zones 4-10. A low-maintenance option with year-round appeal in some areas, Pennisetum plants have finely textured blades and an elegant, mounding form. Their tapering foliage and flower plumes add all-season interest, the perfect easy-care addition to your home landscape. Read more.

The post Learn How to Grow Fountain Grass (Pennisetum) appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: How to Grow and Care For Lewisia Bitterroot (Cliff Maids)
item.description:
Lewisia, also called bitterroot or cliff maids, is a small succulent native to the rocky mountains and outcroppings of western North America. It forms a rosette of thick, fleshy leaves topped with bright flowers in pink, yellow, orange, purple, and white in spring and early summer. Learn how to grow lewisia in this guide.

The post How to Grow and Care For Lewisia Bitterroot (Cliff Maids) appeared first on Gardener's Path.
item.source:markdown:
